<!-- pagina 387 -->

# 5.18 Indication of Track Conditions [¶4389]

# 5.18.1 Introduction [¶4390]

- 5.18.1.1 This  set  of  procedures  specifies  the  sequences  of  driver  indications  related  to  the following track-conditions: [¶4391]

- powerless section with pantograph to be lowered [¶4392]

- powerless section with main power switch to be switched off, [¶4393]

-  non-stopping area, [¶4394]

- radio hole, [¶4395]

- air tightness area, [¶4396]

-  Inhibition of a defined type of brake, [¶4397]

- tunnel stopping area, [¶4398]

- sound horn, [¶4399]

- change of traction system. [¶4400]

- 5.18.1.2 Note:  For  every  procedure  a  figure  supports  the  textual  description.  The  textual description of the procedures contains reference to the figures (point B, point F,…). [¶4401]

# 5.18.2 Passing a powerless section with pantograph to be lowered [¶4402]

- 5.18.2.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  announcement  and  indication  to  the  driver  of  a powerless section with the pantograph to be lowered. [¶4403]

- 5.18.2.1.1  Intentionally deleted. [¶4404]

- 5.18.2.2 'Lower pantograph announcement' shall be displayed to the driver when the max safe front  end  of  the  train  reaches  a  location  (point  C)  in  rear  of  the  beginning  of  the powerless section (point D). [¶4405]

- 5.18.2.2.1  This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4406]

- 5.18.2.2.2  The displayed 'Lower pantograph announcement' information shall also indicate if the related functionality is executed [¶4407]

-  automatically, or [¶4408]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4409]

- 5.18.2.2.2.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4410]

- 5.18.2.3 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the start location (point D) of the powerless section: [¶4411]


---
<!-- pagina 388 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4412]

-  'Lower pantograph announcement' shall no longer be displayed to the driver, [¶4413]

-  'Lowered Pantograph' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4414]

- 5.18.2.4 Intentionally deleted. [¶4415]

- 5.18.2.4.1  Intentionally deleted. [¶4416]

- 5.18.2.5 When the min safe front end of the train reaches the 'Powerless section' end location point E): [¶4417]

-  'Lowered Pantograph' information shall no longer be displayed to the driver [¶4418]

-  'Raise pantograph' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4419]

- 5.18.2.5.1  The  displayed  'Raise  pantograph'  information  shall  also  indicate  if  the  related functionality is executed [¶4420]

-  automatically, or [¶4421]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4422]

- 5.18.2.5.1.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4423]

- 5.18.2.6 The  'Raise  pantograph'  information  shall  remain  displayed  for  a  fixed  time  (see Appendix A.3.1) after the minimum safe rear end of the train has passed the end of the 'Powerless section'. [¶4424]

- 5.18.2.6.1  Note: The train front end position when this information disappears is shown as point G in the Figure 13. [¶4425]

![](images/image_0097.png)

Figure 13: Passing a powerless section with pantograph to be lowered [¶4426]


---
<!-- pagina 389 -->

# 5.18.3 Passing a powerless section with main power switch to be switched off [¶4427]

- 5.18.3.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  announcement  and  indication  to  the  driver  of  a powerless section with main power switch to be switched off. [¶4428]

- 5.18.3.2 'Neutral  section  announcement'  shall  be  displayed  to  the  driver  when  the  max  safe front  end  of  the  train  reaches  a  location  (point  C)  in  rear  of  the  beginning  of  the powerless section. [¶4429]

- 5.18.3.2.1  This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4430]

- 5.18.3.2.2  The  displayed  'Neutral  section  announcement'  information  shall  also  indicate  if  the related functionality is executed [¶4431]

-  automatically, or [¶4432]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4433]

- 5.18.3.2.2.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4434]

- 5.18.3.3 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the starting location (point D) of the powerless section: [¶4435]

-  'Neutral section announcement' shall no longer be displayed to the driver, [¶4436]

-  'Neutral section' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4437]

- 5.18.3.4 When the min safe front end of the train reaches the 'Powerless section' end location point E): [¶4438]

-  'Neutral section' information shall no longer be displayed to the driver [¶4439]

-  'End of Neutral section' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4440]

- 5.18.3.4.1  The  displayed  'End  of  Neutral  section'  information  shall  also  indicate  if  the  related functionality is executed [¶4441]

-  Automatically, or [¶4442]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4443]

- 5.18.3.4.1.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4444]

- 5.18.3.5 The 'End of Neutral section' information shall remain displayed for a fixed time (see Appendix A.3.1) after the minimum safe rear end of the train has passed the end of the 'Powerless section'. [¶4445]

- 5.18.3.5.1  Note: The train front end position, when this information disappears is shown as point G in the Figure 14. [¶4446]


---
<!-- pagina 390 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4447]

![](images/image_0098.png)

Figure 14: Passing a powerless section with main power switch to be switched off [¶4448]

# 5.18.4 Passing a non stopping area [¶4449]

- 5.18.4.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  announcement  and  indication  to  the  driver  of  an area where stopping is not permitted. [¶4450]

- 5.18.4.2 As long as there are non stopping areas stored on-board, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall continuously check the current speed and position of the train whether a  full  service  brake  command  would  stop  the  train  within  any  of  the  stored  non stopping areas. This shall be achieved taking into account for each non stopping area, two virtual SBI supervision limits (SBID and SBIG), calculated at the estimated speed from two SBD curves of which the feet are the start location of the non stopping area (point D) and a location (point G) at train length distance in advance of the end location of the non stopping area: [¶4451]

- If  the  max  safe  front  end  is  in  rear  of  the  first  SBI  supervision  limit  (SBID)  no information related to this non stopping area shall be displayed [¶4452]

- If the max safe front end is in advance of the first SBI supervision limit (SBID) and the min safe front end in rear of the second SBI supervision limit (SBIG), the non stopping area related information shall be displayed to the driver according to the following clauses 5.18.4.3, 5.18.4.4. [¶4453]

-  If the min safe front end is in advance of the second SBI supervision limit (SBIG), no information related to this non stopping area shall be displayed. [¶4454]

- 5.18.4.3 When  the  max  safe  front  end  is  in  rear  of  the  start  location  (point  D)  of  the  non stopping area, the 'Non stopping area announcement' shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4455]

- 5.18.4.4 When the max safe front end is in advance of the start location (point D) of the non stopping area: [¶4456]

-  'Non stopping area announcement', if any, shall no longer be displayed [¶4457]


---
<!-- pagina 391 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4458]

-  'Non stopping area' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4459]

- 5.18.4.5 Intentionally deleted. [¶4460]

- 5.18.4.6 Note: the display of the 'Non stopping area' information will always end before the min safe rear end reaches the point E, because of the condition 5.18.4.2 c). [¶4461]

![](images/image_0099.png)

Figure 15: Passing a non stopping area [¶4462]

# 5.18.5 Passing a radio hole [¶4463]

- 5.18.5.1 This procedure is dealing with the automatic deactivation of the safe radio connection supervision inside an announced radio hole area. [¶4464]

- 5.18.5.2 When the max safe front end of the train passes the start location (point D) of the radio hole area, the 'radio hole' indication shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4465]

- 5.18.5.3 When the min safe rear end of the train passes the end location (point E) of the radio hole area, the 'radio hole' indication to the driver shall be removed. [¶4466]


---
<!-- pagina 392 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4467]

![](images/image_0100.png)

Figure 16: Passing an announced radio hole [¶4468]

# 5.18.6 Passing an 'air tightness' area [¶4469]

- 5.18.6.1 This procedure is dealing with the announcement and indication to the driver of an air tightness area. [¶4470]

- 5.18.6.2 'Close  air  conditioning  intake  announcement'  information  shall  be  displayed  to  the driver when the max safe front end of the train reaches a location (point C) in rear of the beginning of the 'air tightness' area. [¶4471]

- 5.18.6.2.1  This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4472]

- 5.18.6.2.2  The  displayed  'Close  air  conditioning  intake  announcement'  information  shall  also indicate if the related functionality is executed [¶4473]

-  automatically, or [¶4474]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4475]

- 5.18.6.2.2.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4476]

- 5.18.6.3 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the start location (point D) of the air tightness area: [¶4477]

-  'Close  air  conditioning  intake  announcement'  information  shall  no  longer  be displayed. [¶4478]

-  'Air conditioning intake closed' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4479]

- 5.18.6.4 When the min safe rear end of the train reaches the end location (point E) of the air tightness area: [¶4480]

-  'Air conditioning intake closed' information shall no longer be displayed; [¶4481]


---
<!-- pagina 393 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4482]

-  'Open air conditioning intake' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4483]

- 5.18.6.4.1  The  displayed  'Open  air  conditioning  intake'  information  shall  also  indicate  if  the related functionality is executed [¶4484]

-  automatically, or [¶4485]

-  If the driver is requested to act. [¶4486]

- 5.18.6.4.1.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4487]

- 5.18.6.5 The 'Open Air Conditioning intake' information shall remain displayed for a fixed time (see Appendix A.3.1) after the minimum safe rear end of the train has passed the end of the 'Air tightness area'. [¶4488]

- 5.18.6.5.1  Note: The train front end position, when this information disappears, is shown as point G in the Figure 17. [¶4489]

![](images/image_0101.png)

Figure 17: Passing an air tightness area [¶4490]

# 5.18.7 Inhibition of a defined type of brake [¶4491]

- 5.18.7.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  announcement  and  indication  to  the  driver  of  the inhibition of defined types of brake systems. [¶4492]

- 5.18.7.2 The  procedure  shows  the  case  of  the  regenerative  brake.  Regarding  eddy  current brake and magnetic shoe brake, the procedure is identical except that the displayed indications refer respectively eddy current brake or magnetic shoe brake. [¶4493]

- 5.18.7.3 'Inhibition of Regenerative Brake announcement' information shall be displayed to the driver when the max safe front end of the train reaches a location (Point C) in rear of the beginning of the regenerative brake inhibition area. [¶4494]

- 5.18.7.3.1  The displayed 'Inhibition of Regenerative Brake announcement' information shall also indicate if the related functionality is executed [¶4495]


---
<!-- pagina 394 -->

-  automatically, or [¶4496]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4497]

- 5.18.7.3.1.1 Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4498]

- 5.18.7.3.2  This  location  (point  C)  shall  be  determined  by  the  On-Board  equipment  taking  into account the time necessary for performing the required actions and the current train speed. [¶4499]

- 5.18.7.4 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the start location (point D) of the regenerative brake inhibition area: [¶4500]

-  Indication  'Inhibition  of  Regenerative  Brake  announcement'  shall  no  longer  be displayed, [¶4501]

-  Indication 'Inhibition of Regenerative Brake' shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4502]

- 5.18.7.5 When  the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the regenerative  brake  inhibition  area  the  indication  'Inhibition  of  Regenerative  Brake' information shall no longer be displayed. [¶4503]

![](images/image_0102.png)

Figure 18: Passing an area where Regenerative Brake shall be inhibited [¶4504]

# 5.18.8 Advising a tunnel stopping area [¶4505]

- 5.18.8.1 This  procedure is  dealing  with  the  indication  to  the  driver  of  an  area  under  a  tunnel where stopping is permitted, and suitable escaping paths are provided. [¶4506]

- 5.18.8.1.1  Note: the tunnel stopping areas are designed in such a way that the passengers can step out, use a walkway along the track and reach the safe area, taking into account the longest admissible train in the tunnel stopped with its front end within the tunnel stopping area. [¶4507]

- 5.18.8.2 On  driver  request,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  enable/disable  the display of the tunnel stopping area related information (initial state: disabled). [¶4508]


---
<!-- pagina 395 -->

- 5.18.8.3 As long as the display of the tunnel stopping areas is enabled (point C) and there are tunnel  stopping  areas  stored  on-board,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall continuously check the current speed and position of the train to determine whether the driver can stop the train with the full service brake before reaching the end of the closest  tunnel  stopping  area.  This  shall  be  achieved  taking  into  account  a  virtual Permitted supervision limit (with no contribution of the GUI curve, if any), calculated at the estimated speed from an SBD curve of which the foot is the end location of the tunnel stopping area: [¶4509]

- If the train front end is in rear of the Permitted supervision limit, the tunnel stopping area related information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4510]

- If the train front end is in advance of the Permitted supervision limit, no information related to this tunnel stopping area shall be displayed and the next tunnel stopping area stored on-board, if any, shall be checked. [¶4511]

- 5.18.8.4 Before the train reaches the start location (point  D)  of  the  tunnel  stopping area, the 'tunnel  stopping  area  announcement'  shall  be  displayed  to  the  driver.  This  shall include  the  indication  of  the  remaining  distance  to  the  start  location  of  the  tunnel stopping area. [¶4512]

- 5.18.8.5 When the train  front  end  reaches  the  start  location  (point  D)  of  the  tunnel  stopping area: [¶4513]

-  'Tunnel stopping area announcement' shall no longer be displayed [¶4514]

-  'Tunnel stopping area' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4515]

- 5.18.8.6 Note: the display of the 'tunnel stopping area' information will always end before the point E is reached, because of the condition 5.18.8.3 b). [¶4516]

![](images/image_0103.png)

Figure 19: Advising a tunnel stopping area [¶4517]


---
<!-- pagina 396 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4518]

# 5.18.9 Sounding the horn [¶4519]

- 5.18.9.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  indication  to  the  driver  of  a  request  to  sound  the horn. [¶4520]

- 5.18.9.2 'Sound horn' shall be displayed to the driver when the estimated front end of the train reaches a location (point C) in rear of the beginning of the 'sound horn' area (point D). [¶4521]

- 5.18.9.2.1  This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  the  driver  to  perform  the  required  action (see Appendix A.3.1) and the current train speed. [¶4522]

- 5.18.9.3 When the estimated front end of the train  reaches  the  end  location (point  E)  of  the 'sound  horn'  area,  the  indication  'Sound  horn'  information  shall  no  longer  be displayed. [¶4523]

- 5.18.9.4 Note: In order not to encourage the driver to stop sounding the horn too early, the track condition  'sound  horn'  is  defined  as  an  area  so  that  the  display  of  the  indication 'Sound horn' continues during a certain distance sent by trackside (i.e. until point E), after the theoretical location of the board on the line (i.e. point D). [¶4524]

![](images/image_0104.png)

Figure 20: Sounding the Horn [¶4525]

# 5.18.10 Changing the traction system [¶4526]

- 5.18.10.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  announcement  and  indication  to  the  driver  of  a change of traction system. [¶4527]

- 5.18.10.2 'Change of traction system announcement' shall be displayed to the driver when the max safe front end of the train reaches a location (point C) in rear of the location of change of traction system (point F). [¶4528]


---
<!-- pagina 397 -->

- 5.18.10.3 This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4529]

- 5.18.10.4 The  displayed  'Change  of  traction  system  announcement'  information  shall  also indicate if the related functionality is executed [¶4530]

-  automatically, or [¶4531]

-  if the driver is requested to act. [¶4532]

- 5.18.10.4.1  Note: Whether the operation is automatic or manual is application dependent. [¶4533]

- 5.18.10.5 When the  max  safe  front  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  'Change  of  traction  system' location (point F): [¶4534]

-  the  'Change  of  traction  system  announcement'  information  shall  no  longer  be displayed to the driver, [¶4535]

-  the 'New traction system' information shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4536]

- 5.18.10.6 The  'New  traction  system'  information  shall  remain  displayed  for  a  fixed  time  (see Appendix A.3.1) after the minimum safe rear end of the train has passed the 'Change of traction system' location (point F). [¶4537]

- 5.18.10.6.1  Note: The train front end position when this information disappears is shown as point G in the Figure 21. [¶4538]

![](images/image_0105.png)

Figure 21: Changing the traction system [¶4539]


---
<!-- pagina 398 -->

# 5.19 Procedure Limited Supervision [¶4540]

# 5.19.1 General Requirements [¶4541]

- 5.19.1.1 The order to switch to Limited Supervision mode shall be given by means of a mode profile. [¶4542]

- 5.19.1.2 An acknowledgement for running in Limited Supervision mode shall be requested from the driver. The conditions of the acknowledgement are specified below. [¶4543]

# 5.19.2 Limited Supervision is requested for current location (from modes different from Stand By and Post Trip) [¶4544]

- 5.19.2.1 In  a  level  1  area,  the  beginning  of  the  Limited  Supervision  area  can  be  the  balise (group)  that  gives  the  Mode  Profile.  When  the  train  passes  the  balise  group  and receives  this  information,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  immediately switch to Limited Supervision mode. [¶4545]

- 5.19.2.2 In  a  level  2  or  3  area,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  can  receive  a  mode profile  giving  a  Limited  Supervision  area  which  the  train has already entered with its max  safe  front  end.  In  this  case,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall immediately switch to Limited Supervision mode. [¶4546]

- 5.19.2.3 The driver must acknowledge the Limited Supervision mode. A request of acknowledgement shall be displayed to the driver. [¶4547]

- 5.19.2.4 If  the  driver  has  not  acknowledged  after  the  driver  acknowledgement  time  (refer  to Appendix A.3.1), the service brake command shall be triggered. The brake command is  released  when  the  driver  acknowledges,  except  if  brakes  are  also  applied  for another reason(s). [¶4548]

- 5.19.2.5 Note : Once in Limited Supervision mode, the speed supervision is such that the train speed  cannot  exceed  the  LS  mode  speed  limit.  If,  when  entering  the  Limited Supervision mode, the train speed was higher than the LS mode speed limit (because a  higher  speed  was  allowed  in  Full  Supervision  mode,  On  Sight  mode  or  in  Staff Responsible  mode)  then  an  emergency  brake  command  could  be  immediately triggered, independently of the acknowledgement of the driver, but because of the LS supervision (see Figure 22). [¶4549]


---
<!-- pagina 399 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4550]

![](images/image_0106.png)

# Figure 22: Train enters LS area with too high speed [¶4551]

- 5.19.2.6 Note : This sharp brake reaction can be avoided in Full Supervision or On Sight mode by  giving  with  the  previous  MA  an  EOA  (or  a  LOA  =  LS  mode  speed  limit)  at  the location of transition to Limited Supervision mode. [¶4552]

- 5.19.2.7 If the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is already in LS mode when receiving the LS mode profile, no further acknowledgement shall be requested from the driver. [¶4553]

# 5.19.3 Limited Supervision is requested for a further location [¶4554]

- 5.19.3.1 The beginning of the Limited Supervision area can be a location that the train has not reached yet. This occurs when: [¶4555]

- In a level 1 area, a balise group gives a Mode Profile with an Limited Supervision area that is located at a further location. [¶4556]

- In  a  level  2  or  3  area,  the  RBC  gives  a  Mode  Profile with an Limited Supervision area that is located at a further location. [¶4557]

- 5.19.3.2 A  request  for  acknowledgement  shall  be  displayed  to  the  driver  when  the  following conditions are fulfilled: [¶4558]

-  The  distance  between  the  estimated  front  end  of  the  train  and  the  beginning  of Limited Supervision area is shorter than a value, contained in the mode profile. [¶4559]

- The speed is lower than the Limited Supervision mode speed limit (national value, or value given in the mode profile). [¶4560]

-  The current mode is not Limited Supervision [¶4561]

- 5.19.3.3 Note : The first 2 conditions define the 'rectangle of acknowledgement'. [¶4562]

- 5.19.3.4 Once the acknowledgement request is displayed, it shall not be taken back if the train leaves the 'rectangle of acknowledgement' (for example: because the train accelerates). [¶4563]


---
<!-- pagina 400 -->

- 5.19.3.5 Until the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment has switched to LS mode,  the beginning of the Limited Supervision area shall be temporarily considered as the EOA (instead of the  EOA/LOA  derived  from  the  MA)  and  the  SvL  (with  no  release  speed)  shall  be determined according to the mode profile either: [¶4564]

- as the SvL derived from the MA. In case the MA defines an LOA, the SvL shall be derived as if no LOA had been given. [¶4565]

OR [¶4566]

- as the beginning of the Limited Supervision area. [¶4567]

- 5.19.3.6 When the driver acknowledges the Limited Supervision mode, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall immediately switch to the Limited Supervision mode. [¶4568]

![](images/image_0107.png)

# Figure 23: Transition to LS mode after driver acknowledgement [¶4569]

- 5.19.3.7 If the max safe front end of the train reaches the beginning of the Limited Supervision area  according  the  mode  profile  and  the  driver  has  not  yet  acknowledged,  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall switch immediately to LS mode and a request for  acknowledgement  shall  be  displayed  to  the  driver  (refer  to  SRS  chapter  4, transitions between modes). [¶4570]

- 5.19.3.8 If,  in  this  case,  the  driver  does  not  acknowledge  within  the  driver  acknowledgement time  (refer  to  Appendix  A.3.1)  after  the  change  to  LS  mode,  the  service  brake command shall be triggered. The command shall be released as soon as the driver acknowledges (unless the command was triggered also for other reasons). [¶4571]


---
<!-- pagina 401 -->

# 5.19.4 Limited Supervision from Unfitted or SN mode [¶4572]

- 5.19.4.1 The mode profile with regards to an LS area is only evaluated in levels 1,2,3, although the  mode  profile  may  have  been  received  in  level  0  (Unfitted  mode)  or  NTC  (SN mode).  A  transition  to  Limited  Supervision  mode  can  therefore  earliest  occur  at  a transition of level: from level 0 or NTC to level 1 or 2 or 3. [¶4573]

- 5.19.4.2 Requirements about the acknowledgement in section 5.19.2 shall apply. [¶4574]

# 5.19.5 Limited Supervision from Stand By or Post Trip mode [¶4575]

- 5.19.5.1 When performing a SoM or a Train Trip procedure and when the current level is 2 or 3, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment can receive a mode profile giving an Limited Supervision area which the train has already entered with its max safe front end. In this case, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall first require an acknowledgement from the driver. [¶4576]

- 5.19.5.2 When the driver acknowledges, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall perform the transition to Limited Supervision mode. [¶4577]

# 5.19.6 Exit of Limited Supervision mode [¶4578]

# 5.19.6.1 General rule [¶4579]

- 5.19.6.1.1  The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall exit the Limited Supervision mode when the min safe front end of the train passes the end of the Limited supervision area. [¶4580]

# 5.19.6.2 First case: The Limited supervision area ends at the EOA/LOA of the current MA [¶4581]

- 5.19.6.2.1  This occurs when the end of the Limited Supervision area that is given by the Mode Profile has the same location as the EOA/LOA of the related MA. [¶4582]

- 5.19.6.2.2  In this case, the train must receive a new Movement Authority to be able to exit the Limited Supervision area. [¶4583]

# 5.19.6.3 Second  case:  The  Limited  Supervision  area  ends  before  the  EOA/LOA  of  the current MA [¶4584]

- 5.19.6.3.1  In this case, the current Movement Authority already allows the train to exit the Limited Supervision area. [¶4585]

- 5.19.6.3.2  When  exiting  the  Limited  Supervision  area,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment switches  either  to  Full  Supervision,  On  Sight  or  to  Shunting  mode  (refer  to  SRS chapter 4, transitions between modes). [¶4586]


---
<!-- pagina 402 -->

# 5.19.7 Flowchart [¶4587]

![](images/image_0108.png)

Figure 24: Flowchart for 'Limited Supervision' [¶4588]

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4589]


---
<!-- pagina 403 -->

# 5.20 Generation of Track Conditions related information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function [¶4590]

# 5.20.1 Introduction [¶4591]

- 5.20.1.1 This set of procedures specifies the information generated by the ERTMS/ETCS onboard  equipment  to  an  ERTMS/ETCS  external  function  in  relation  to  the  following track-conditions: [¶4592]

- powerless section with pantograph to be lowered, [¶4593]

- powerless section with main power switch to be switched off, [¶4594]

-  passing an 'air tightness' area, [¶4595]

- inhibition of a defined type of brake, [¶4596]

- change of traction system, [¶4597]

-  change of allowed current consumption, [¶4598]

- station platform. [¶4599]

- 5.20.1.2 In the following sections, the remaining distance from the concerned train end to the concerned track condition location shall be counted positive when the train end is in rear of the location and shall be counted negative when the train end is in advance of the location. [¶4600]

# 5.20.2 Passing a powerless section with pantograph to be lowered [¶4601]

- 5.20.2.1 This procedure is dealing with the generation of the information related to a powerless section with the pantograph to be lowered. [¶4602]

- 5.20.2.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches the point C in rear of the start location of the powerless section (see 5.18.2.2.1 for the definition of point C). [¶4603]

- 5.20.2.3 When the min safe rear end of the train is in rear of the start location (point D) of the powerless section, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall provide the following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4604]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the start location (point D) of the powerless section [¶4605]

-  the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the end location (point E) of the powerless section [¶4606]

- 5.20.2.4 When the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  start  location  (point  D)  of  the powerless section, the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to point D shall no longer be generated. The remaining distance from the min safe front [¶4607]


---
<!-- pagina 404 -->

end of the train to the end location (point E) of the powerless section shall continue to be generated. [¶4608]

- 5.20.2.5 When  the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the powerless section, the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to point E shall no longer be generated. [¶4609]

- 5.20.2.6 Note:  The  remaining  distance  from  the  train  max  safe  front  end  to  point  D  and  the remaining distance from the train min safe front end to point E are generated until the min  safe  rear  end  passes  respectively  the  point  D  and  the  point  E  to  allow  the ERTMS/ETCS external function to manage each pantograph individually assuming that this function knows the distance between each pantograph and the train front end. [¶4610]

- 5.20.2.7 Note:  When  resuming  the  initial  state  (see  3.7.3.2)  or  any  other  reason  (e.g.  see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the shortening of a powerless section with pantograph to be lowered, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment considers the new end location (point E) of the powerless section for the calculation of the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the point E. [¶4611]

- 5.20.2.8 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the deletion of the powerless section, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall stop providing information related to this powerless section to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4612]

![](images/image_0109.png)

Figure 25: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of powerless section with pantograph to be lowered [¶4613]

# 5.20.3 Passing a powerless section with main power switch to be switched off [¶4614]

- 5.20.3.1 This procedure is dealing with the generation of the information related to a powerless section with main power switch to be switched off. [¶4615]


---
<!-- pagina 405 -->

- 5.20.3.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches the point C in rear of the start location of the powerless section (see 5.18.3.2.1 for the definition of point C). [¶4616]

- 5.20.3.3 When the min safe rear end of the train is in rear of the start location (point D) of the powerless section, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall provide the following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4617]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the start location (point D) of the powerless section [¶4618]

-  the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the end location (point E) of the powerless section [¶4619]

- 5.20.3.4 When the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  start  location  (point  D)  of  the powerless section, the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to point D shall no longer be generated. The remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the end location (point E) of the powerless section shall continue to be generated. [¶4620]

- 5.20.3.5 When  the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the powerless section, the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to point E shall no longer be generated. [¶4621]

- 5.20.3.6 Note:  The  remaining  distance  from  the  train  max  safe  front  end  to  point  D  and  the remaining distance from the train min safe front end to point E are generated until the min  safe  rear  end  passes  respectively  the  point  D  and  the  point  E  to  allow  the ERTMS/ETCS  external  function  to  manage  the  main  power  switch  of  each  vehicle individually assuming that this function knows the distance between each vehicle and the train front end. [¶4622]

![](images/image_0110.png)

Figure 26: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of powerless section with main power switch to be switched off [¶4623]


---
<!-- pagina 406 -->

- 5.20.3.7 Note:  When  resuming  the  initial  state  (see  3.7.3.2)  or  any  other  reason  (e.g.  see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the shortening of a powerless section with main power switch to  be  switched  off,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  considers  the  new  end location (point E) of the powerless section for the calculation of the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the point E. [¶4624]

- 5.20.3.8 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the deletion of the powerless section, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall stop providing information related to this powerless section to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4625]

# 5.20.4 Passing an 'air tightness' area [¶4626]

- 5.20.4.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  generation  of  the  information  related  to  an  air tightness area. [¶4627]

- 5.20.4.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches the point C in rear of the start location of the air tightness area (see 5.18.6.2.1 for the definition of point C). [¶4628]

- 5.20.4.3 When the max safe front end of the train is in rear of the start location (point D) of the air  tightness  area, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall provide the following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4629]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the start location (point D) of the air tightness area [¶4630]

-  the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to the end location (point E) of the air tightness area [¶4631]

- 5.20.4.4 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the start location (point D) of the air tightness area, the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to point D shall no longer be generated. The remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the  train  to  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the  air  tightness  area  shall  continue  to  be generated. [¶4632]

- 5.20.4.5 When the min safe rear end of the train reaches the end location (point E) of the air tightness area, the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to point E shall no longer be generated. [¶4633]


---
<!-- pagina 407 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4634]

![](images/image_0111.png)

Figure 27: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for an air tightness area [¶4635]

- 5.20.4.6 Note:  When  resuming  the  initial  state  (see  3.7.3.2)  or  any  other  reason  (e.g.  see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the shortening of an air tightness area, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment considers the new end location (point E) of the air tightness area for the calculation of the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to the point E. [¶4636]

- 5.20.4.7 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the deletion of the air tightness area, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall stop providing information related to this air tightness area to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4637]

# 5.20.5 Inhibition of a defined type of brake [¶4638]

- 5.20.5.1 This procedure is dealing with the generation of the information related to the inhibition of defined types of brake systems. [¶4639]

- 5.20.5.2 The  procedure  shows  the  case  of  the  regenerative  brake.  Regarding  eddy  current brake and magnetic shoe brake, the procedure is identical except that the generated information refers to respectively eddy current brake or magnetic shoe brake. [¶4640]

- 5.20.5.3 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches the point C in rear of the start location of the regenerative brake inhibition area (see 5.18.7.3.2 for the definition of point C). [¶4641]

- 5.20.5.4 When the max safe front end of the train is in rear of the start location (point D) of the regenerative  brake  inhibition  area,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall provide the following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4642]


---
<!-- pagina 408 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4643]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the start location (point D) of the regenerative brake inhibition area [¶4644]

-  the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to the end location (point E) of the regenerative brake inhibition area [¶4645]

- 5.20.5.5 When the max safe front end of the train reaches the start location (point D) of the regenerative brake inhibition area, the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to point D shall no longer be generated. The remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to the end location (point E) of the regenerative brake inhibition area shall continue to be generated. [¶4646]

- 5.20.5.6 When  the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the regenerative brake inhibition area, the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to point E shall no longer be generated. [¶4647]

- 5.20.5.7 Note:  When  resuming  the  initial  state  (see  3.7.3.2)  or  any  other  reason  (e.g.  see Appendix  A.3.4)  leads  to  the  shortening  of  a  regenerative  brake  inhibition  area,  the ERTMS/ETCS on-board  equipment  considers  the  new  end  location  (point  E)  of  the regenerative brake inhibition area for the calculation of the remaining distance from the min safe rear end of the train to the point E. [¶4648]

- 5.20.5.8 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the deletion of the regenerative brake inhibition area, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard  equipment  shall  stop  providing  information  related  to  this  regenerative  brake inhibition area to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4649]

![](images/image_0112.png)

Figure 28: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of regenerative brake inhibition area [¶4650]

# 5.20.6 Changing the traction system [¶4651]

- 5.20.6.1 This procedure is dealing with the generation of the information related to a change of traction system. [¶4652]


---
<!-- pagina 409 -->

- 5.20.6.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches the point C in rear of the location of change of traction system (see 5.18.10.3 for the definition of point C). [¶4653]

- 5.20.6.3 When the min safe rear end of the train is in rear of the location of change of traction system  (point  F),  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  provide  the  following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4654]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the location of change of traction system (point F) [¶4655]

-  the identity of the new traction system [¶4656]

- 5.20.6.4 When the min safe rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  location  of  change  of  traction system (point F), the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to point F and the identity of the new traction system shall no longer be generated. [¶4657]

- 5.20.6.5 Note: The remaining distance from the train max safe front end to point F is generated until  the  min  safe  rear  end  passes  the  point  F  to  allow  the  ERTMS/ETCS  external function,  in  case  the  change  of  traction  system  involves  several  pantographs,  to manage each pantograph individually assuming that this function knows the distance between each pantograph and the train front end. [¶4658]

- 5.20.6.6 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads  to  the  deletion  of  the  change  of  traction  system,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board equipment shall stop providing information related to this change of traction system to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4659]

![](images/image_0113.png)

Figure 29: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of change of traction system [¶4660]


---
<!-- pagina 410 -->

# 5.20.7 Changing the allowed current consumption [¶4661]

- 5.20.7.1 This procedure is dealing with the generation of the information related to a change of allowed current consumption. [¶4662]

- 5.20.7.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches a location  (point  C)  in  rear  of  the  location  of  change  of  allowed  current  consumption (point F). [¶4663]

- 5.20.7.3 This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4664]

- 5.20.7.4 When the min safe rear end of the train is in rear of the location of change of allowed current  consumption  (point  F),  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  provide the following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4665]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the location of allowed current consumption (point F) [¶4666]

-  the new allowed current consumption [¶4667]

- 5.20.7.5 When the min safe rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  location  of  change  of  allowed current consumption (point F), the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the  train  to  point  F  and  the  new  allowed  current  consumption  shall  no  longer  be generated. [¶4668]

- 5.20.7.6 Note: The remaining distance from the train max safe front end to point F is generated until  the  min  safe  rear  end  passes  the  point  F  to  allow  the  ERTMS/ETCS  external function, in case  the change  of  allowed  current consumption  involves  several pantographs,  to  manage  each  pantograph  individually  assuming  that  this  function knows the distance between each pantograph and the train front end. [¶4669]

![](images/image_0114.png)


---
<!-- pagina 411 -->

# Figure 30: Generation of the information to an ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of change of allowed current consumption [¶4670]

- 5.20.7.7 If any reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the deletion of the change of allowed current  consumption,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  stop  providing information related to this change of allowed current consumption to the ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4671]

# 5.20.8 Station platform [¶4672]

- 5.20.8.1 This  procedure  is  dealing  with  the  generation  of  the  information  related  to  a  station platform. [¶4673]

- 5.20.8.2 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall start the generation of the information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function when the max safe front end of the train reaches a location (point C) in rear of the start location of the station platform (point D). [¶4674]

- 5.20.8.3 This location (point C) shall be determined by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment taking  into  account  the  time  necessary  for  performing  the  required  actions  and  the current train speed. [¶4675]

- 5.20.8.4 When the min safe rear end of the train is in rear of the start location (point D) of the station  platform,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  shall  provide  the  following information to the ERTMS/ETCS external function: [¶4676]

-  the remaining distance from the max safe front end of the train to the start location (point D) of the station platform [¶4677]

-  the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the end location (point E) of the station platform [¶4678]

-  the nominal height of platform above rail level (refer to TSI infrastructure) [¶4679]

-  the position of the station platform (left side, right side, both sides) in reference to the train orientation [¶4680]

- 5.20.8.5 When the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  start  location  (point  D)  of  the station  platform,  the  remaining  distance  from  the  max  safe  front  end  of  the  train  to point D shall no longer be generated. The remaining distance from the min safe front end  of  the  train  to  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the  station  platform  as  well  as  its nominal height above the rail level and its position shall continue to be generated. [¶4681]

- 5.20.8.6 When  the  min  safe  rear  end  of  the  train  reaches  the  end  location  (point  E)  of  the station platform, the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to point E, the position of the station platform and its nominal height above the rail level shall no longer be generated. [¶4682]

- 5.20.8.7 Note:  The  remaining  distance  from  the  train  max  safe  front  end  to  point  D  and  the remaining distance from the train min safe front end to point E are generated until the min  safe  rear  end  passes  respectively  the  point  D  and  the  point  E  to  allow  the [¶4683]


---
<!-- pagina 412 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4684]

ERTMS/ETCS external function to manage each door individually assuming that this function knows the distance between each door and the train front end. [¶4685]

![](images/image_0115.png)

Figure 31: Generation of the information to ERTMS/ETCS external function for a supervision of station platform [¶4686]

- 5.20.8.8 Note:  When  resuming  the  initial  state  (see  3.7.3.2)  or  any  other  reason  (e.g.  see Appendix A.3.4) leads to the shortening of a station platform, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment considers the new end location (point E) of the station platform for the calculation of the remaining distance from the min safe front end of the train to the point E. [¶4687]

- 5.20.8.9 If resuming the initial state (see 3.7.3.2) or any other reason (e.g. see Appendix A.3.4) leads  to  the  deletion  of  the  station  platform,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment shall  stop  providing  information  related  to  this  station  platform  to  the  ERTMS/ETCS external function. [¶4688]

# 6.3 Scope [¶4704]

- 6.3.1.1 The chapter defines the composition of envelope of legally operated system versions, i.e.  all  the  ERTMS/ETCS  system  versions  that  trackside  shall  be  allowed  to  operate and that on-board equipment shall support. [¶4705]

- 6.3.1.2 By default, all the clauses listed in the other SRS chapters are applicable regardless of the  system  version  operated  (see  SUBSET-104  §  6.1.3.2);  this  chapter  includes  the exceptions to those clauses and the additional clauses, which apply when the system version of some trackside constituents and/or the system version operated relates to a version number older than the last one introduced. [¶4706]


---
<!-- pagina 418 -->

# 6.4 Envelope of legally operated system versions [¶4707]

# 6.4.1 Incompatible versions [¶4708]

- 6.4.1.1 The system version number X, which a trackside infrastructure is allowed to operate with, shall be one of the following: 1 or 2 [¶4709]

# 6.4.2 Compatible versions [¶4710]

- 6.4.2.1 Within  system  version  number  X  =  1,  the  system  version  number  Y  that  a  trackside infrastructure is allowed to use shall be any of the following: 0 or 1 [¶4711]

- 6.4.2.2 Within  system  version  number  X  =  2,  the  system  version  number  Y  that  a  trackside infrastructure is allowed to use shall be any of the following: 0 or 1 [¶4712]


---
<!-- pagina 419 -->

# 6.5 Trackside requirements in relation to older system versions [¶4713]

# 6.5.1 Trackside areas operated with system version number X = 1 [¶4714]

# 6.5.1.1 Introduction [¶4715]

- 6.5.1.1.1 The  section  is  applicable  for  trackside  infrastructures  that  will  be  tendered  and  still operated with the system version number X = 1, after the entry into force of this release of the SRS. [¶4716]

- 6.5.1.1.2 Within a trackside infrastructure operated with the system version number X = 1, it shall be allowed to use the following values of M_VERSION: 1.0, and 1.1 [¶4717]

- 6.5.1.1.3 Within a trackside area operated with an RBC certified to the system version number X = 1, it shall also be allowed to use for balises the following values of M_VERSION: 2.0 and 2.1. [¶4718]

- 6.5.1.1.3.1  Note:  this  configuration  is  meaningful  in  case  the  trains  operating  on  this  RBC  area support the system version number X = 2 and the on-board requirements related to the trackside information marked with 2.0 or 2.1 are applicable regardless of the operated version (i.e. they are applied by the on-board equipment even if this latter operates with the system version number X = 1 ordered by RBC). [¶4719]

# 6.5.1.2 Exceptions to chapter 3 [¶4720]

- 6.5.1.2.1 Intentionally deleted. [¶4721]

- 6.5.1.2.1.1  Section 3.6.2.4 shall not apply. [¶4722]

- 6.5.1.2.2 Clause  3.7.1.1  b)  shall  be  replaced  with:  'When  needed,  limitations  related  to  the movement authority, i.e. Mode profile for On Sight or Shunting and signalling related speed restriction (see sections 3.12.4 and 3.11.6). Mode profile and Signalling related Speed restriction shall always be sent together with the MA to which the information belongs' [¶4723]

- 6.5.1.2.3 In clause 3.7.1.1 c), the bullet 'Optionally Speed restriction to ensure a given permitted braking distance (see section 3.11. 11)' shall not apply. [¶4724]

- 6.5.1.2.4 In clause 3.7.2.4, the bullet 'LX speed restrictions' shall not apply. [¶4725]

- 6.5.1.2.5 In  clause  3.7.2.4,  the  bullet  'Inhibition  of  revocable  TSRs  from  balises  in  L2/3  (from RBC only)' shall not apply. [¶4726]

- 6.5.1.2.6 Clause 3.9.3.2 shall be replaced with: 'The orders shall be sent via balise groups.' [¶4727]

- 6.5.1.2.7 Clause 3.9.3.8.1 shall not apply. [¶4728]

- 6.5.1.2.8 Clause 3.11.3.2.2 c) shall not apply. [¶4729]


---
<!-- pagina 420 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4730]

- 6.5.1.2.9 Clause 3.11.3.2.3.1 shall be replaced with: 'If at least one other specific SSP is less restrictive  than  any  'Cant  Deficiency'  SSP,  it  is  the  responsibility  of  the  trackside engineering to ensure that for all possible combinations of international train categories a  train  might  belong  to,  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  will  not  replace  the 'Cant  Deficiency'  SSP  as  selected  in  3.11.3.2.3  leading  to  an  unsafe  situation  by applying the requirement 3.11.3.2.6' [¶4731]

- 6.5.1.2.10  Clause 3.11.5.12 shall not apply. [¶4732]

- 6.5.1.2.11  Clauses 3.11.9.1, 3.12.5.1, 3.12.5.2, 3.12.5.4, 3.12.5.5, 3.12.5.6, 3.12.5.7, 3.15.1.2.3.1 o) shall not apply. [¶4733]

- 6.5.1.2.12  Clauses 3.11.11.1, 3.11.11.2 shall not apply [¶4734]

- 6.5.1.2.13  Clause 3.12.3.4.3.2 shall not apply. [¶4735]

- 6.5.1.2.14  Clauses 3.12.3.1.11 and 3.12.3.5.1 shall not apply. [¶4736]

- 6.5.1.2.15  Clause  3.12.4.1  shall  be  replaced  with:  'It  shall  be  possible  for  trackside  to  send  a Mode Profile. The Mode Profile can request On Sight mode and Shunting mode.' [¶4737]

- 6.5.1.2.16  Intentionally deleted. [¶4738]

- 6.5.1.2.17  Clause 3.15.1.2.3.1 p) shall not apply. [¶4739]

- 6.5.1.5.36  Note: the packets listed above, which are not allowed for use in balise telegrams/loop messages marked with system version number X = 1 or in messages from RBC/RIU operating  with  system  version  number  X  =  1,  may  contain  variables  that  have  been introduced in the system version number X = 2. These variables are not mentioned in this  section,  since  their  use  is  implicitly  forbidden  by  the  fact  that  the  packets  using them are not allowed. [¶4868]

# 6.5.1.6 Exceptions to chapter 8 [¶4869]

- 6.5.1.6.1 Clause 8.4.1.4.5 shall not apply. [¶4870]

- 6.5.1.6.2 Clause  8.4.1.4.8  shall  be  replaced  with  'Exception  8:  A  message  transmitted  by  a balise group can contain several packets 200 (Virtual Balise Cover marker).' [¶4871]

- 6.5.1.6.3 The table under clause 8.4.2.1 shall be replaced with: [¶4872]


---
<!-- pagina 441 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4873]

[¶4874]
| General Format of Balise Telegram   | General Format of Balise Telegram   | General Format of Balise Telegram   |                                                                                                                                  |
|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Field No.                           | VARIABLE                            | Length ( bits)                      | Remarks                                                                                                                          |
| 1                                   | Q_UPDOWN                            | 1                                   | Defines the direction of the information: Down-link telegram (train to track) (0) Up-link telegram (track to train) (1)          |
| 2                                   | M_VERSION                           | 7                                   | Version of the ERTMS/ETCS system.                                                                                                |
| 3                                   | Q_MEDIA                             | 1                                   | Defines the type of media: Balise (0)                                                                                            |
| 4                                   | N_PIG                               | 3                                   | Position in the group. Defines the position of the balise in the balise group.                                                   |
| 5                                   | N_TOTAL                             | 3                                   | Total number of balises in the balise group                                                                                      |
| 6                                   | M_DUP                               | 2                                   | Used to indicate whether the information of the balise is a duplicate of the balise before or after this one.                    |
| 7                                   | M_MCOUNT                            | 8                                   | Message counter (M_MCOUNT) - 8 bits. To enable detection of a change of balise group message during passage of the balise group. |
| 8                                   | NID_C                               | 10                                  | Country or region.                                                                                                               |
| 9                                   | NID_BG                              | 14                                  | Identity of the balise group.                                                                                                    |
| 10                                  | Q_LINK                              | 1                                   | Marks the balise group as linked (Q_LINK = 1) or unlinked (Q_LINK = 0)                                                           |
|                                     | Packet 200 (optional)               | 29                                  | Virtual Balise Cover marker                                                                                                      |
|                                     | Information                         | Variable                            | This information is composed according to the rules applicable for packets.                                                      |
|                                     | Packet 255                          | 8                                   | Finishing flag of the telegram                                                                                                   |

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4900]

- 6.5.1.7.1 Any balise telegram, which includes the packet 2, the packet 6, the packet 135, the packet  145,  the  packet  200,  the  packet  203,  the  packet  206,  the  packet  207  or  the packet 239, shall be marked with the system version number 1.1. [¶4901]

- 6.5.1.7.2 An RBC that uses the packet 203, the packet 206, the packet 207 or the packet 239 shall transmit a system version number equal to 1.1, when negotiating the establishment of the communication session. [¶4902]

- 6.5.1.7.3 Any message transmitted by loop, which includes the packet 206, the packet 207 or the packet 239, shall be marked with the system version number 1.1. [¶4903]

- 6.5.1.7.4 An RIU that uses the packet 206, the packet 207 or the packet 239 shall transmit a system  version  number  equal  to  1.1,  when  negotiating  the  establishment  of  the communication session. [¶4904]

- 6.5.1.7.5 A balise group or RBC message including the packet 203 shall also include the packet 3 (i.e. in a message, the packet 203 cannot be transmitted without the packet 3). [¶4905]

- 6.5.1.7.6 In  the  packet  70,  the  use  of  the  value  '00'  of  the  variable  Q_SUITABILITY  shall  be forbidden. [¶4906]

# 6.5.2 Trackside areas operated with system version number X = 2 [¶4907]

# 6.5.2.1 General [¶4908]

- 6.5.2.1.1 This  section  is  applicable  for  trackside  infrastructures  that  were  operated  with  the system version number X = 1, before the migration to the system version number X = 2. [¶4909]

- 6.5.2.1.1.1  This section is also applicable for trackside infrastructures with RBCs/RIUs certified to the system version number X.Y = 2.0 or for trackside infrastructures with RBCs/RIUs certified  to  the  system  version  number  X.Y  =  2.1  where  trains  are  operated  with  onboard equipment whose highest supported system version number X.Y is only 2.0. [¶4910]

- 6.5.2.1.2 Within a trackside infrastructure operated with the system version number X = 2, it shall be allowed to use the following values of M_VERSION: 1.0, 1.1, 2.0 and 2.1 [¶4911]

# 6.5.2.2 Exceptions to chapter 3, 4, 5 [¶4912]

- 6.5.2.2.1 For  RBCs  certified  to  the  system  version  number  2.0  and  for  RBCs  certified  to  the system  version  number  2.1  handing  over  a  train  fitted  with  an  on-board  equipment whose  highest  supported  system  version  number  X.Y  is  only  2.0,  the  bullet  'The system versions supported by the on-board equipment' in clause 3.15.1.2.1 b) shall not apply. [¶4913]

# 6.5.2.3 Exceptions to chapter 7, 8 [¶4914]

- 6.5.2.3.1 For the balise telegrams/loop messages marked with the system version number 1.0 or 1.1 and for messages transmitted by RIUs certified to the system version number 1.0 or 1.1, the exceptions listed in sections 6.5.1.5 and 6.5.1.6 shall apply by analogy. [¶4915]

# 6.6 On-board requirements in relation to older system versions [¶4933]

# 6.6.1 Introduction [¶4934]

- 6.6.1.1 This section covers the following situations: [¶4935]

- Train is running on a trackside infrastructure operated with system version number X = 1 [¶4936]

- Train is running on a trackside infrastructure operated with system version number X = 2, but still transmitting some balise/loop/RIU information related to system version number X = 1 (see section 6.5.2) [¶4937]

-  Train is running on a trackside infrastructure operated with system version number X =  2,  but  on-board  equipment  has  established  a  communication  session  with  a neighbouring RBC certified to system version number X = 1 [¶4938]

- Train is running on a trackside infrastructure operated with system version number X = 1 or 2 and the on-board equipment has established a communication session with a RBC/RIU certified to system version number X.Y = 2.0 [¶4939]

# 6.6.2 Specific requirements for on-board operating with system version number X = 1 [¶4940]

# 6.6.2.1 Exceptions to chapter 3 [¶4941]

- 6.6.2.1.1 Clause 3.12.3.4.7.2 shall be replaced with: 'If the driver acknowledges before the end condition is fulfilled, the on-board equipment shall consider the driver acknowledgement as always ending the text display, regardless of the end condition defined in 3.12.3.4.3.1'. [¶4942]

# 6.6.2.2 Exceptions to chapter 4 [¶4943]

- 6.6.2.2.1 Clause 4.4.11.1.3 d) shall be replaced with: 'balise groups giving the order 'stop if in SR'. This order shall immediately trip the train.' [¶4944]

- 6.6.2.2.2 Condition [54] in table 4.6.3 shall be replaced with: '(reception of information 'stop if in Staff Responsible') AND (override is not active)'. [¶4945]

# 6.6.2.3 Exceptions to chapter 5 [¶4946]

# 6.6.2.3.1 Void [¶4947]

# 6.6.2.4 Exceptions to chapter 7, 8 [¶4948]

# 6.6.2.4.1 Void [¶4949]

# 6.6.3 Handling of air gap data related to system version number X = 1 [¶4950]

# 6.6.3.1 General [¶4951]


---
<!-- pagina 448 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶4952]

- 6.6.3.1.1 For  information  received  from  trackside,  the  message  consistency  check  shall  be achieved  taking  into  account  the  exceptions  to  chapters  7  and  8,  as  described  in sections 6.5.1.5 and 6.5.1.6. [¶4953]

- 6.6.3.1.2 For information received from trackside, the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment shall use the translation tables defined here below, in order to use the information as if it had been elaborated in compliance with the current chapters 7 and 8. [¶4954]

# 6.6.3.2 Packets received from balise, loop, RIU, RBC [¶4955]

- 6.6.3.2.1 In the table below, the translation of information may depend on the on-board operated system version at the time the information is received and accepted on-board. [¶4956]

- 6.6.3.2.2 When a level transition or an RBC/RBC handover is announced, the information stored on-board  in  the  transition  buffer  shall  be  translated  according  to  system  version operated on-board at the time the information is released from the transition buffer (i.e. the system version operated by the trackside infrastructure, towards which the train is running). [¶4957]

- 6.6.3.2.3 Depending on the packet, the action can be: [¶4958]

- data is unchanged, [¶4959]

- data is rejected, [¶4960]

-  data is translated, [¶4961]

- not relevant [¶4962]

R = Rejected T = Translated U = Unchanged NR = Not relevant [¶4963]

[¶4964]
| Received information   | Received information         | Action                               | Action                               |
|------------------------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| Packet Number          | Packet Name                  | Operated system version number X = 1 | Operated system version number X = 2 |
| 2                      | System Version Order         | U                                    | U                                    |
| 3                      | National Values              | T [1a] [2]                           | T [1b] [2]                           |
| 5                      | Linking                      | U                                    | U                                    |
| 6                      | Virtual Balise Cover order   | U                                    | U                                    |
| 12                     | Level 1 Movement Authority   | U                                    | U                                    |
| 15                     | Level 2/3 Movement Authority | U                                    | U                                    |
| 16                     | Repositioning Information    | U                                    | U                                    |
| 21                     | Gradient Profile             | U                                    | U                                    |

[1a] The National Values Q_NVLOCACC, V_NVLIMSUPERV (introduced in system version number X = 2) shall be set to their respective default value [¶4968]

[1b] The National Values Q_NVLOCACC, V_NVLIMSUPERV (introduced in system version number X = 2), if already stored on-board and applicable, shall not be affected by the content of the packet 3 (i.e. if these National Values were already applicable and 2 nd bullet of clause 3.18.2.5 is not applied, they shall remain applicable with their country identifier(s) previously stored). [¶4969]

[2]  If  the  packet  203  is  not  received  in  the  same  message,  the  National  Values  for  braking  curves Q_NVGUIPERM, Q_NVSBFBPERM, Q_NVINHSMICPERM, M_NVAVADH, M_NVEBCL, A_NVP12, A_NVP23,  V_NVKVINT,  M_NVKVINT,  L_NVKRINT,  M_NVKRINT,  M_NVKTINT,  A_NVMAXREDADH1, A_NVMAXREDADH2, A_NVMAXREDADH3 (introduced in  system  version  number  X.Y  =  1.1),  if  already stored  on-board  and  applicable,  shall  not  be  affected  by  the  content  of  the  packet  3  (i.e.  if  the  National Values for braking curves were already applicable and 2 nd bullet of clause 3.18.2.5 is not applied, they shall remain applicable with their country identifier(s) previously stored). [¶4970]

[3]  Exception:  if  N_ITER  (following  Q_FRONT)  0,  the  variables  Q_DIFF,  NC_CDDIFF  (introduced  in system version number X = 2) and NC_DIFF (as specified in system version number X = 2) shall be set according to the following table: [¶4971]

[10] Within the 8 digits of the variable NID_OPERATIONAL (as specified in system version X = 1), any digit coded as "F" shall be rightmost shifted, in order to obtain a variable NID_OPERATIONAL (as specified in system version X = 2) made of the significant digits leftmost adjusted followed by digits coded as "F". [¶4983]

[11] Exception: If the packet 207 is not received in the same message and if Q_TRACKINIT = 1, the packet shall not be rejected. [¶4984]

[12] Exception: If the packet 207 is not received in the same message and if the value '10' of the variable Q_SUITABILITY is used with M_TRACTION equal to one of the values that are listed in the translation table [13], the variables M_VOLTAGE and NID_CTRACTION (introduced in system version X.Y = 1.1) shall be set according to the translation table [13]. The ERTMS/ETCS on-board shall ignore any other route suitability information not related to the traction system [¶4985]

-  The  variable  NID_PACKET shall be set to 0 and both the variables Q_DIR and L_PACKET shall be deleted [¶4991]

- The National Values included in the packet 203 shall be appended to the packet 3 received in the same message, in order to form a single set of National Values, to which apply the distance to start of validity and the list of national area identifiers given in the packet 3. [¶4992]

# 6.6.3.3 Messages received from RBC/RIU [¶4996]

- 6.6.3.3.1 This section applies for the parts of radio messages,  excluding the packets themselves,  which  are  received  from  an  RBC/RIU  certified  to  the  system  version number X = 1. [¶4997]

- 6.6.3.3.2 Depending on the received message, the action can be: [¶4998]

- data is unchanged, [¶4999]

- data is rejected [¶5000]

-  data is translated, [¶5001]

- not relevant [¶5002]

R = Rejected [¶5003]

T = Translated [¶5004]

U = Unchanged [¶5005]

NR = Not relevant [¶5006]

[¶5007]
|   Messag e Number | Message Name                       | Action   |
|-------------------|------------------------------------|----------|
|                 2 | SR Authorisation                   | U        |
|                 3 | Movement Authority                 | U        |
|                 6 | Recognition of exit from TRIP mode | U        |
|                 8 | Acknowledgement of Train Data      | U        |
|                 9 | Request to Shorten MA              | U        |
|                15 | Conditional Emergency Stop         | T [1]    |


---
<!-- pagina 454 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶5008]

[¶5009]
|   Messag e Number | Message Name                                              | Action   |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|----------|
|                16 | Unconditional Emergency Stop                              | U        |
|                18 | Revocation of Emergency Stop                              | U        |
|                24 | General message                                           | U        |
|                27 | SH Refused                                                | U        |
|                28 | SH Authorised                                             | U        |
|                33 | MA with Shifted Location Reference                        | U        |
|                34 | Track Ahead Free Request                                  | T [1]    |
|                37 | Infill MA                                                 | U        |
|                40 | Train Rejected                                            | U [2]    |
|                32 | RBC/RIU system version                                    | U        |
|                39 | Acknowledgement of termination of a communication session | U        |
|                41 | Train Accepted                                            | U [2]    |
|                43 | SoM position report confirmed by RBC                      | U        |
|                45 | Assignment of coordinate system                           | U        |

[1] Variable D_REF (introduced in system version number X = 2) shall be set to 0

[2] Exception: if the variable NID_LRBG ≠ 16777215, it shall be set to 16777215, i.e. the message shall be considered as having been received with NID_LRBG = 16777215.

Note: An RBC X=1 based on an older version of the specification may use a value different from 16777215 for NID_LRBG in message 40 or 41. [¶5010]

# 6.6.3.4 Messages transmitted to RBC/RIU [¶5011]

- 6.6.3.4.1 This section applies for radio messages/packets, which are transmitted to an RBC or an RIU certified to the system version number X = 1. [¶5012]

- 6.6.3.4.1.1  Clause  3.5.3.7  d)  1 st bullet  shall  be  replaced  with:  'If  one  of  its  supported  system versions  is  compatible  with  the  one  sent  by  trackside,  it  shall  send  a  session established report, including its telephone numbers, to the trackside.' [¶5013]

- 6.6.3.4.1.2  Clause 3.12.3.5.2 shall not apply. [¶5014]

- 6.6.3.4.2 Clause 3.18.4.5.4 shall be replaced with: 'Only if valid Train Data is available: following any entry/modification of the train running number when a communication session is already  established  or  following  the  successful  establishment  of  a  communication session when valid train running number is already available, the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment shall send the Train Data to the RBC. [¶5015]


---
<!-- pagina 455 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶5016]

- 6.6.3.4.3 Clause 3.18.4.5.4.1 shall be replaced with: 'Exception: if the train running number has been received from the RBC, the Train Data shall not be sent back to the RBC by the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment. [¶5017]

- 6.6.3.4.4 The ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment shall elaborate the information to be transmitted to the RBC/RIU certified to system version number X = 1, by applying the following  translation  table  to  the  corresponding  information  intended  for  an  RBC/RIU certified to the system version number X.Y = 2.1. [¶5018]

- 6.6.3.4.5 Depending on the transmitted message/packet, the action can be: [¶5019]

- data is unchanged, [¶5020]

- data is deleted (i.e. it is not sent to the receiver) [¶5021]

-  data is translated, [¶5022]

- not relevant (i.e. no corresponding requirement to trigger the sending is applicable) [¶5023]

# D = Deleted T = Translated U = Unchanged NR = Not relevant [¶5024]

| XXX 5            | Train Running Number                       | NR       |
