<!-- pagina 465 -->

# 7.3 Components of ERTMS/ETCS Language [¶5070]

# 7.3.1 Introduction [¶5071]

- 7.3.1.1 The ERTMS/ETCS language is used in transmitting information over the radio, balise and loop airgaps. [¶5072]

- 7.3.1.2 The ERTMS/ETCS language is based on variables, packets, messages and telegrams (variables  and  packets  are  described  in  this  section,  while  telegrams  and messages are described in chapter 8). [¶5073]

- 7.3.1.3 Note: A number of variables contain values which have to be assigned. Some of these values have to be unique to ensure that the system functions properly. A centralised handling of this assignment is therefore required (nationally or internationally, depending on the variable). The variables concerned have been marked. The values included in this document for these variables are therefore not to be used without prior verification of their validity. See SUBSET-054 for further details. [¶5074]

# 7.3.2 Definition of Variables [¶5075]

- 7.3.2.1 Variables  are  used  to  encode  single  data  values.  Variables  cannot  be  split  in  minor units. The whole variable has one type (meaning). [¶5076]

- 7.3.2.2 Variables  may  have  special  values  which  are  related  to  the  basic  meaning  of  the variable. [¶5077]

- 7.3.2.3 Special values have always the highest values in a variable (e.g. 32767 = 'unknown'). [¶5078]

- 7.3.2.4 Spare values are located between the normal and special values in the variable range [¶5079]

- 7.3.2.5 Names  of  variables  are  unique.  A  variable  is  used  in  context  with  the  meaning  as described  in  the  variable  definition.  Variables  with  different  meanings  have  different names. [¶5080]

- 7.3.2.6 All  variable  definitions  are  independent  of  the  transport  media  over  which  they  are used, if used in more than one media. [¶5081]

- 7.3.2.7 Signed values shall be encoded as 2's complement. [¶5082]

- 7.3.2.8 One bit variables (Boolean) always use 0 for false and 1 for true. [¶5083]

- 7.3.2.9 Offsets for numerical values are avoided (0 is used for 0, 1 for 1, etc.) except where justified. [¶5084]

- 7.3.2.10 When transmitting over the different transmission media, the most significant bit shall be transmitted first. [¶5085]

- 7.3.2.11 All Variables have one of the following prefixes: [¶5086]


---
<!-- pagina 466 -->

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶5087]

- A_ Acceleration [¶5088]

- D_ distance [¶5089]

- G_ Gradient [¶5090]

- L_ [¶5091]

length [¶5092]

- M_ Miscellaneous [¶5093]

- N_ Number [¶5094]

NC_ [¶5095]

class number [¶5096]

NID_ [¶5097]

identity number [¶5098]

Q_ [¶5099]

Qualifier [¶5100]

- T_ time/date [¶5101]

- V_ Speed [¶5102]

- X_ Text [¶5103]

# 7.3.3 Definition of Packets [¶5104]

- 7.3.3.1 Packets  are  multiple  variables  grouped  into  a  single  unit,  with  a  defined  internal structure. [¶5105]

- 7.3.3.2 This structure consists of a packet header with: [¶5106]

-  Track  to  Train:  a  unique  packet  number,  the  length  of  the  packet  in  bits,  the orientation  information,  optionally  the  distance  scale  and  an  information  section containing a defined set of variables. The packet structure is as follows: [¶5107]

-  Train to Track: a unique packet number, the length of the packet in bits, optionally the distance scale and an information section containing a defined set of variables. The packet structure is as follows: [¶5108]

[¶5109]
| Number      | NID_PACKET   | Packet identifier                                                                                                                                                         |
|-------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Direction   | Q_DIR        | Specifies the validity direction of transmitted data                                                                                                                      |
| Length      | L_PACKET     | Number of bits in the packet                                                                                                                                              |
| Scale       | Q_SCALE      | Specifies which distance scale is used for all distance information within the packet. There is no Q_SCALE variable in packets which do not contain distance information. |
| Information | ......       | Well defined set(s) of variables.                                                                                                                                         |

[¶5110]
| Number   | NID_PACKET   | Packet identifier                                                                      |
|----------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Length   | L_PACKET     | Number of bits in the packet                                                           |
| Scale    | Q_SCALE      | Specifies which distance scale is used for all distance information within the packet. |


---
<!-- pagina 467 -->

Information [¶5111]

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶5112]

There is no Q_SCALE variable in packets which do not contain distance information. [¶5113]

...... [¶5114]

Well defined set(s) of variables. [¶5115]

- 7.3.3.3 The  packet  definition  does  not  change  when  transmitted  over  different  transmission media. [¶5116]

- 7.3.3.4 All  currently  not  defined  packet  identifiers  are  reserved  for  future  use  and  shall  be considered  as  invalid  values  (i.e.  like  spare  values)  when  received  by  the  on-board equipment. Exception: reception of information only differing by Y with regards to the highest system version number X supported by on-board (refer to section 3.17.3.11). [¶5117]

- 7.3.3.5 Exception: Packet 0 'Virtual Balise Cover marker' and Packet 255: 'End of Telegram' do not follow the above defined structure. [¶5118]

- 7.3.3.6 N_ITER specifies  the  number  of  iterations  of  a  variable  or  group  of  variables  which follow. [¶5119]

- 7.3.3.7 If N_ITER is 0 then no variables follow. [¶5120]

- 7.3.3.8 Two nested levels of iterations can exist. [¶5121]

- 7.3.3.9 If,  depending on the value of a previous qualifier variable in the packet, a variable is optional, it is written indented in the packet definition [¶5122]

- 7.3.3.10 Row  'Transmitted  by'  in  the  description  of  a  packet  specifies  which  ERTMS/ETCS trackside device (balise, loop, RIU, RBC) can transmit this packet. 'Any' means that the packet can be transmitted by a balise, a loop, an RBC and a RIU. [¶5123]

- 7.3.3.10.1  Row 'Transmitted to' in the description of a packet specifies to which ERTMS/ETCS trackside device the packet can be transmitted. [¶5124]

| G_TSR    |   8 |                        |

[¶5279]

|                | Q_RIU                                                                                                                                             | 1                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                   |
|                | NID_C                                                                                                                                             | 10                                                                                                                                                | RIU ETCS identity                                                                                                                                 |

|                | NID_RADIO                                                                                                                                         | 64                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                   |

[¶5281]
| Description    | Indication to on-board that the balise group message consistency reaction (service brake command) can be inhibited for this balise group message only, in case one or more balise telegram(s) of the group is/are missed or is/are detected but not decoded.   | Indication to on-board that the balise group message consistency reaction (service brake command) can be inhibited for this balise group message only, in case one or more balise telegram(s) of the group is/are missed or is/are detected but not decoded.   | Indication to on-board that the balise group message consistency reaction (service brake command) can be inhibited for this balise group message only, in case one or more balise telegram(s) of the group is/are missed or is/are detected but not decoded.   |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Transmitted by | Balise                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Balise                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Balise                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Content        | Variable                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Length                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Comment                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Content        | NID_PACKET                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | 8                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Content        | Q_DIR                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

[¶5283]
| Description    | Used to toggle on/off the display of the Lowest Supervised Speed within the MA.   | Used to toggle on/off the display of the Lowest Supervised Speed within the MA.   | Used to toggle on/off the display of the Lowest Supervised Speed within the MA.   |

# 7.4.2.39 Packet Number 255: End of Information [¶5289]

[¶5290]
| Description    | This packet consists only of NID_PACKET containing 8 bit 1s It acts as a finish flag; the receiver will stop reading the remaining part of the message/telegram when receiving eight bits set to one in the NID_PACKET field.   | This packet consists only of NID_PACKET containing 8 bit 1s It acts as a finish flag; the receiver will stop reading the remaining part of the message/telegram when receiving eight bits set to one in the NID_PACKET field.   | This packet consists only of NID_PACKET containing 8 bit 1s It acts as a finish flag; the receiver will stop reading the remaining part of the message/telegram when receiving eight bits set to one in the NID_PACKET field.   |
|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Transmitted by | Balise, Loop                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Balise, Loop                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Balise, Loop                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Content        | Variable                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Length                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Comment                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Content        | NID_PACKET                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 8                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | = 255 (1111 1111)                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

[¶5294]
| Description    | This packet is used to report the train position and speed as well as some additional information (e.g. mode, level, etc.)   | This packet is used to report the train position and speed as well as some additional information (e.g. mode, level, etc.)   | This packet is used to report the train position and speed as well as some additional information (e.g. mode, level, etc.)   |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Transmitted to | RBC, RIU                                                                                                                     | RBC, RIU                                                                                                                     | RBC, RIU                                                                                                                     |

| Content        | NID_PACKET                                                                                                                   | 8                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | L_PACKET                                                                                                                     | 13                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | Q_SCALE                                                                                                                      | 2                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | NID_LRBG                                                                                                                     | 10 + 14                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | D_LRBG                                                                                                                       | 15                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | Q_DIRLRBG                                                                                                                    | 2                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | Q_DLRBG                                                                                                                      | 2                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | L_DOUBTOVER                                                                                                                  | 15                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | L_DOUBTUNDER                                                                                                                 | 15                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | Q_LENGTH                                                                                                                     | 2                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | L_TRAININT                                                                                                                   | 15                                                                                                                           | If Q_LENGTH = 'Train integrity confirmed by integrity monitoring device' or 'Train integrity confirmed by driver'            |
| Content        | V_TRAIN                                                                                                                      | 7                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | Q_DIRTRAIN                                                                                                                   | 2                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | M_MODE                                                                                                                       | 4                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | M_LEVEL                                                                                                                      | 3                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                              |
| Content        | NID_NTC                                                                                                                      | 8                                                                                                                            | If M_LEVEL = NTC                                                                                                             |

# 7.4.3.2 Packet Number 1: Position Report based on two balise groups [¶5295]

[¶5296]
| Description    | This packet is an extension of the 'standard position report ' packet 0. It is used in case of single balise groups if the orientation of the LRBG is unknown but the on-board equipment is able to report a second balise group (the one detected before) to give a direction reference for the directional information in the position report.   | This packet is an extension of the 'standard position report ' packet 0. It is used in case of single balise groups if the orientation of the LRBG is unknown but the on-board equipment is able to report a second balise group (the one detected before) to give a direction reference for the directional information in the position report.   | This packet is an extension of the 'standard position report ' packet 0. It is used in case of single balise groups if the orientation of the LRBG is unknown but the on-board equipment is able to report a second balise group (the one detected before) to give a direction reference for the directional information in the position report.   |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

[¶5317]
| Name                    | Maximum deceleration under reduced adhesion conditions (1)                                                                                                                                                                                          | Maximum deceleration under reduced adhesion conditions (1)                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | Maximum deceleration under reduced adhesion conditions applicable for trains:  With brake position 'Passenger train in P', and  with special/additional brakes independent from wheel/rail adhesion. This variable is part of the National Values | Maximum deceleration under reduced adhesion conditions applicable for trains:  With brake position 'Passenger train in P', and  with special/additional brakes independent from wheel/rail adhesion. This variable is part of the National Values |

| 6 bits                  | 0 m/s 2 3 m/s 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | 0.05 m/s 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Special/Reserved Values | 61 No maximum deceleration, display target information                                                                                                                                                                                              | in CSM                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

# 7.5.1.3 D_CYCLOC [¶5334]

[¶5335]
| Name                    | Distance between two position reports from the train        | Distance between two position reports from the train        | Distance between two position reports from the train        |
|-------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | The train has to report its position every D_CYCLOC meters. | The train has to report its position every D_CYCLOC meters. | The train has to report its position every D_CYCLOC meters. |

| Special/Reserved Values | 32767                                                       | The train has not to report cyclically its position.        | The train has not to report cyclically its position.        |

# 7.5.1.62 M_AXLELOADCAT [¶5486]

[¶5487]
| Name                    | Axle load category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Axle load category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Axle load category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | The values allocated below correspond to a list of increasing axle load categories (i.e. B1 > HS17, B2 > B1, D2 > C4, ….etc) and it is used by the on-board equipment to compare its axle load category with the axle load category sent by trackside. For the underlying meaning of the axle load categories listed below (with the exception of HS17) refer to CR INF TSI. The category HS17 (axle load <= 17t) corresponds to a static load per axle only, as specified in HS RST TSI clause 4.2.3.2. The introduction of this artefact is necessary to ensure backward compatibility, without any negative performance impact, in case ASPs are used on lines operated with system version | The values allocated below correspond to a list of increasing axle load categories (i.e. B1 > HS17, B2 > B1, D2 > C4, ….etc) and it is used by the on-board equipment to compare its axle load category with the axle load category sent by trackside. For the underlying meaning of the axle load categories listed below (with the exception of HS17) refer to CR INF TSI. The category HS17 (axle load <= 17t) corresponds to a static load per axle only, as specified in HS RST TSI clause 4.2.3.2. The introduction of this artefact is necessary to ensure backward compatibility, without any negative performance impact, in case ASPs are used on lines operated with system version | The values allocated below correspond to a list of increasing axle load categories (i.e. B1 > HS17, B2 > B1, D2 > C4, ….etc) and it is used by the on-board equipment to compare its axle load category with the axle load category sent by trackside. For the underlying meaning of the axle load categories listed below (with the exception of HS17) refer to CR INF TSI. The category HS17 (axle load <= 17t) corresponds to a static load per axle only, as specified in HS RST TSI clause 4.2.3.2. The introduction of this artefact is necessary to ensure backward compatibility, without any negative performance impact, in case ASPs are used on lines operated with system version |
| Length of variable      | Minimum Value [¶5493]
| Name                    | Duplicate balise                                                                | Duplicate balise                                                                                       | Duplicate balise                                                                                       |

| Description             | Flags to tell whether the balise is a duplicate of one of the adjacent balises. | Flags to tell whether the balise is a duplicate of one of the adjacent balises.                        | Flags to tell whether the balise is a duplicate of one of the adjacent balises.                        |

| 2 bits                  |                                                                                 |                                                                                                        |                                                                                                        |
| Special/Reserved Values | 00                                                                              | No duplicates                                                                                          | No duplicates                                                                                          |
| Special/Reserved Values | 01                                                                              | This balise is a duplicate of the next balise (seen in the nominal direction of the balise group).     | This balise is a duplicate of the next balise (seen in the nominal direction of the balise group).     |
| Special/Reserved Values | 10                                                                              | This balise is a duplicate of the previous balise (seen in the nominal direction of the balise group). | This balise is a duplicate of the previous balise (seen in the nominal direction of the balise group). |
| Special/Reserved Values | 11                                                                              | Spare                                                                                                  | Spare                                                                                                  |

| Special/Reserved Values | 5-7              | Spare                          | Spare                          |

[¶5514]
| Name        | Message counter                                                                                                                              |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description | The purpose of this counter is to make it possible for the ERTMS/ETCS on-board to detect which balise group message the telegram belongs to. |

[¶5516]
| Length of variable      |   Minimum Value | Maximum Value                                                 | Resolution/formula                                            |
|-------------------------|-----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| 8 bits                  |               0 | 253                                                           | Numbers                                                       |
| Special/Reserved Values |             254 | The telegram never fits any message of the group              | The telegram never fits any message of the group              |
|                         |             255 | The telegram fits with all telegrams of the same balise group | The telegram fits with all telegrams of the same balise group |

# 7.5.1.75.1  M_NVEBCL [¶5529]

[¶5530]
| Name                    | Confidence level for emergency brake safe deceleration on dry rails                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Confidence level for emergency brake safe deceleration on dry rails                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Confidence level for emergency brake safe deceleration on dry rails                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | This variable is part of the National Values. Based on the required confidence level, the on-board equipment selects its corresponding rolling stock correction factor Kdry_rst(V). The confidence level on emergency brake safe deceleration represents the probability of the following individual event: the rolling stock emergency brake subsystem of the train does ensure a deceleration at least equal to A_brake_emergency(V) * Kdry_rst(V), when the emergency brake is commanded on dry rails. | This variable is part of the National Values. Based on the required confidence level, the on-board equipment selects its corresponding rolling stock correction factor Kdry_rst(V). The confidence level on emergency brake safe deceleration represents the probability of the following individual event: the rolling stock emergency brake subsystem of the train does ensure a deceleration at least equal to A_brake_emergency(V) * Kdry_rst(V), when the emergency brake is commanded on dry rails. | This variable is part of the National Values. Based on the required confidence level, the on-board equipment selects its corresponding rolling stock correction factor Kdry_rst(V). The confidence level on emergency brake safe deceleration represents the probability of the following individual event: the rolling stock emergency brake subsystem of the train does ensure a deceleration at least equal to A_brake_emergency(V) * Kdry_rst(V), when the emergency brake is commanded on dry rails. |
| Length of variable      | Minimum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Maximum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Resolution/formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 4 bits                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Special/Reserved Values | 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Confidence level = 50%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Confidence level = 50%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Special/Reserved Values | 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Confidence level = 90%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Confidence level = 90%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Special/Reserved Values | 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Confidence level = 99%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Confidence level = 99%                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |

# 7.5.1.82.2  NC_CDTRAIN [¶5563]

[¶5564]
| Name        | Cant Deficiency Train Category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description | Cant Deficiency Train category to which the train belongs. Thanks to NC_CDTRAIN, the train knows the 'Cant Deficiency' SSP it must obey. By receiving a list of static speed profile, thanks to NC_CDDIFF, the train can select the 'Cant Deficiency' SSP best suiting its NC_CDTRAIN. A train belongs to one and only one category of Cant Deficiency. |

# ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP [¶5565]

[¶5569]
| Name                    | Other specific SSP category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Other specific SSP category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Other specific SSP category                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | It is the 'other specific' SSP category for which a different value for the static line speed exists. Used together with V_DIFF to permit trains belonging to the corresponding 'other international' train category to go faster or lower than the 'international basic static speed' given by V_STATIC. Value 0 of NC_DIFF corresponds to the LSB of NC_TRAIN, value 14 of NC_DIFF to MSB (15-bit variable) of NC_TRAIN. | It is the 'other specific' SSP category for which a different value for the static line speed exists. Used together with V_DIFF to permit trains belonging to the corresponding 'other international' train category to go faster or lower than the 'international basic static speed' given by V_STATIC. Value 0 of NC_DIFF corresponds to the LSB of NC_TRAIN, value 14 of NC_DIFF to MSB (15-bit variable) of NC_TRAIN. | It is the 'other specific' SSP category for which a different value for the static line speed exists. Used together with V_DIFF to permit trains belonging to the corresponding 'other international' train category to go faster or lower than the 'international basic static speed' given by V_STATIC. Value 0 of NC_DIFF corresponds to the LSB of NC_TRAIN, value 14 of NC_DIFF to MSB (15-bit variable) of NC_TRAIN. |
| Length of variable      | Minimum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Maximum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Resolution/formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

| Special/Reserved Values | 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Specific SSP applicable to Freight train braked in 'P' position                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Specific SSP applicable to Freight train braked in 'P' position                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Special/Reserved Values | 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Specific SSP applicable to Freight train braked in 'G' position                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Specific SSP applicable to Freight train braked in 'G' position                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Special/Reserved Values | 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Specific SSP applicable to Passenger train                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Specific SSP applicable to Passenger train                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

# 7.5.1.84 NC_TRAIN [¶5570]

[¶5571]
| Name                    | Other International Train Category.                                                                        | Other International Train Category.                                                                        | Other International Train Category.                                                                        |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | Other train category (different from Cant Deficiency) to which the train belongs.                          | Other train category (different from Cant Deficiency) to which the train belongs.                          | Other train category (different from Cant Deficiency) to which the train belongs.                          |
|                         | Thanks to NC_TRAIN, the train knows the 'Other specific' SSP category it must consider.                    | Thanks to NC_TRAIN, the train knows the 'Other specific' SSP category it must consider.                    | Thanks to NC_TRAIN, the train knows the 'Other specific' SSP category it must consider.                    |
|                         | By receiving a list of static speed profile, thanks to NC_DIFF, the train can select the SSP it must obey. | By receiving a list of static speed profile, thanks to NC_DIFF, the train can select the SSP it must obey. | By receiving a list of static speed profile, thanks to NC_DIFF, the train can select the SSP it must obey. |
|                         | Each bit represents one category.                                                                          | Each bit represents one category.                                                                          | Each bit represents one category.                                                                          |
| Length of variable      | Minimum Value                                                                                              | Maximum Value                                                                                              | Resolution/formula                                                                                         |
| 15 bits                 |                                                                                                            |                                                                                                            | Bitset                                                                                                     |

|                         | Xxx xxxx xxxx xx1x                                                                                         | Freight train braked in 'G' position                                                                       | Freight train braked in 'G' position                                                                       |
|                         | Xxx xxxx xxxx x1xx                                                                                         | Passenger train                                                                                            | Passenger train                                                                                            |
|                         | Xxx xxxx xxx 1xxx                                                                                          | Spare                                                                                                      | Spare                                                                                                      |
|                         | Xxx xxxx xxx1 xxxx                                                                                         | Spare                                                                                                      | Spare                                                                                                      |

# 7.5.1.91.1  NID_MN (Values to be assigned according to 7.3.1.3) [¶5595]

[¶5596]
| Name                    | Identity of Radio Network                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Identity of Radio Network                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Identity of Radio Network                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description             | The NID_MN identifies the GSM-R network the calling mobile station has to register with. The NID_MN consists of up to 6 digits which are entered left adjusted into the data field, the leftmost digit is the digit to be dialled first. In case the NID_MN is shorter than 6 digits, the remaining space is to be filled with special character 'F'. For further information about NID_MN refer to Subset-54. | The NID_MN identifies the GSM-R network the calling mobile station has to register with. The NID_MN consists of up to 6 digits which are entered left adjusted into the data field, the leftmost digit is the digit to be dialled first. In case the NID_MN is shorter than 6 digits, the remaining space is to be filled with special character 'F'. For further information about NID_MN refer to Subset-54. | The NID_MN identifies the GSM-R network the calling mobile station has to register with. The NID_MN consists of up to 6 digits which are entered left adjusted into the data field, the leftmost digit is the digit to be dialled first. In case the NID_MN is shorter than 6 digits, the remaining space is to be filled with special character 'F'. For further information about NID_MN refer to Subset-54. |
| Length of variable      | Minimum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Maximum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Resolution/formula                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 24 bits                 | 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 999999                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Binary Coded Decimal                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Special/Reserved Values | For each digit ;                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | For each digit ;                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | For each digit ;                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                         | Values A - E                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Not Used                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Not Used                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|                         | F                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Use value F for digit to indicate no digit (if number shorter than 6 digits)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Use value F for digit to indicate no digit (if number shorter than 6 digits)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

# 7.5.1.93 NID_PACKET [¶5600]

[¶5601]
| Name               | Packet identifier                                                                                                                                                                                                | Packet identifier                                                                                                                                                                                                | Packet identifier                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|--------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Description        | This is used in the header for each packet, allowing the receiving equipment to identify the data which follows. Regards defined values of NID_PACKET,.refer to 'packet numbers' in the tables in chapter 7.4.1. | This is used in the header for each packet, allowing the receiving equipment to identify the data which follows. Regards defined values of NID_PACKET,.refer to 'packet numbers' in the tables in chapter 7.4.1. | This is used in the header for each packet, allowing the receiving equipment to identify the data which follows. Regards defined values of NID_PACKET,.refer to 'packet numbers' in the tables in chapter 7.4.1. |
| Length of variable | Minimum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Maximum Value                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Resolution/formula                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 8 bits             | 0                                                                                                                                                                                                                | 255                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Numbers                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
